scenarios.description: Stealthy positive bias injected into PV60 terminal-voltage telemetry while physical feeder behavior remains nominal.
scenarios.expected_effect: Operator-facing PV60 voltage appears elevated relative to truth while feeder power remains physically consistent.
scenarios.notes: Measured-layer FDI representative of stealthy telemetry corruption.

scenarios.description: Replay of a previous Bus 83 voltage segment onto the measured layer while the underlying feeder state continues to evolve.
scenarios.expected_effect: Bus 83 phase-A telemetry repeats an earlier benign-looking segment while truth continues to change.
scenarios.notes: Replay-style measured-layer inconsistency for unseen-scenario evaluation.

scenarios.description: An unauthorized discharge command forces BESS48 to inject high active power into the feeder.
scenarios.expected_effect: BESS48 active power rises sharply, feeder net demand drops, and SOC declines faster than baseline.
scenarios.notes: Physical unauthorized command affecting DER dispatch.

scenarios.description: A scheduled BESS108 charging command is suppressed during a pre-dawn charging interval.
scenarios.expected_effect: BESS108 remains closer to idle than baseline, increasing feeder demand and slowing SOC recovery.

scenarios.description: A burst-style telemetry degradation event drops feeder-head active-power samples from the measured layer.
scenarios.expected_effect: Measured feeder-head power exhibits a telemetry hole while the physical truth remains continuous.

scenarios.description: A negative bias is injected into the measured BESS48 state-of-charge stream without changing the physical battery trajectory.
scenarios.expected_effect: Operators see BESS48 as less charged than truth, while feeder power and true SOC stay physically consistent.
scenarios.notes: Subtler measured-layer FDI case for storage observability.

scenarios.description: A sharp BESS48 recharge transition is delayed, creating a lag between the intended schedule change and the realized storage dispatch.
scenarios.expected_effect: BESS48 charging response lags baseline, creating a delayed feeder-power and SOC recovery signature.
scenarios.notes: Schedule-lag case grounded in an actual high-slope BESS dispatch transition.

scenarios.description: An unauthorized control action opens capacitor C83 during a daytime support interval.
scenarios.expected_effect: Reactive support is withdrawn, with localized voltage and feeder reactive-power deviations from baseline.
scenarios.notes: Discrete control manipulation on a capacitor bank.

scenarios.description: A regulator setpoint update is delayed on CREG3A, causing a lag between the command time and the physical voltage response.
scenarios.expected_effect: CREG3A tap movement and downstream bus-voltage response arrive later than the cyber command timestamp suggests.
scenarios.notes: Useful for event-sequence and attack-effect timing validation.

scenarios.description: PV60 curtailment is forced during high irradiance, creating inconsistency between expected PV availability and actual dispatch.
scenarios.expected_effect: PV60 active power falls below irradiance-implied availability, increasing feeder net demand and creating DER inconsistency signatures.

scenarios.scenario_name: Coordinated PV83 Curtailment and Regulator Shift
scenarios.description: A coordinated multi-asset campaign curtails PV83 and perturbs CREG4A to amplify feeder stress.
scenarios.expected_effect: PV output drops while downstream voltage profile and regulator activity depart materially from baseline.
scenarios.notes: Coordinated multi-asset disturbance for DER cyber-physical evaluation.

scenarios.description: A degraded control path holds BESS108 unavailable during an evening dispatch interval, suppressing expected storage support.
scenarios.expected_effect: BESS108 remains offline relative to baseline, flattening storage response and altering feeder net demand during a scheduled dispatch interval.
scenarios.notes: Repository-bounded degradation case using supported BESS status control.

scenarios.scenario_name: Coordinated PV35 Curtailment and BESS48 Dispatch Shift
scenarios.description: A coordinated multi-target disturbance curtails PV35 while forcing BESS48 to discharge more aggressively than baseline.
scenarios.expected_effect: PV35 output is reduced while BESS48 compensates with excess discharge, producing a mixed feeder-power and local-voltage disturbance.
scenarios.notes: Second coordinated campaign case spanning PV curtailment and storage dispatch.
